engineering.translation: The yoked one, the knower-of-truth (tattva-vit), thinks 'I do nothing at all' (naiva kiñcit karomīti). While seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, breathing — Krishna enumerates the bodily operations across this verse and the next — the seer knows: 'the senses (indriyāṇi) operate among sense-objects (indriyārtheṣu).' The engineering analog stays at the decoupled-identity layer. The senior watches her own attention move toward Slack, toward Datadog, toward the bad performance review — and does not collapse into the attention-movement. The senses operate; the principal is not the operating senses. This is not literal cessation; it is the metaphysical observation that the doer-position is empty. STRETCHED honestly where the passage's transcendent claim exceeds the operational layer; the operational layer holds at decoupled identity, the metaphysical layer reaches further.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A staff engineer at a Kubernetes-shop joins a high-stakes incident at 2:14 AM. PagerDuty fires. She wakes, walks to the laptop, logs in, reads the Datadog dashboards, scans the Sentry traces, runs the rollback per the runbook, watches the latency curve recover, files the GitHub incident issue, types the all-hands Slack update, sleeps at 2:50. Across the thirty-six minutes of the response, her attention has been on Datadog, then Sentry, then the rollback runbook, then the latency-recovery curve, then the GitHub issue, then the Slack message. The senses-among-sense-objects rotation is observable to her as it happens; she does not collapse identity into any one of them. She is not the dashboard, not the rollback, not the Slack message. The work proceeds with full focus; the doer-position is empty. The verse's 'I do nothing' is not vocational — she has done the work — it is a structural observation that the actor-as-identified-self is not present.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader hears 'I do nothing' as a vocational license — 'I shouldn't run the rollback, I shouldn't take responsibility for the incident.' The verse is the opposite: the verse describes the interior posture of the one who is doing the most. The senses operate; the work proceeds; what is absent is the identification with the doer.
engineering.counter_example: When an engineer claims 'I do nothing at all' while genuinely doing nothing — while the work is left to others, the on-call gap is unfilled, the merge queue stalled — the verse does not apply. The verse's claim is the seer's observation during full action; the absent-from-work engineer is not the verse's tattva-vit. They are 3.6's mithyācāra in the inverse.
engineering.implication: During your next high-stakes incident, observe whether identity collapses into the rollback-command or stays at the decoupled-identity layer. The verse's claim is testable in that thirty-second window.
engineering.quotable_line: The senses operate among sense-objects. The senior is fully present, fully responsive, and not identified with any of it.

Cross-references to chapters 2-4 preserved.
